gaps: No test covers app crash during DFU initiation for CC (Charge Case) or CS (Charge Stand) sub-devices on any Poly Bluetooth audio device.
No test covers the stability/resilience of the Poly Studio Mobile app when a DFU process is started for a sub-device component (as opposed to the primary headset/earbuds).
No test covers the specific UI flow of navigating to Firmware Update from Device Settings and initiating a sub-device DFU to verify the app does not crash.
No test covers DFU initiation for sub-devices (CC/CS) on devices like Voyager Focus 2 or similar CC/CS-capable headsets.
No test covers app crash recovery or error handling when a DFU process for a sub-device is triggered.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CC-001: Verify app does not crash when initiating DFU for Charge Case (CC) from Device Settings
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android device.
2. Ensure a CC/CS-capable device (e.g., Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC) is connected via Bluetooth.
3. Tap the connected [Device Name] to open Device Settings screen.
4. Tap 'Firmware Update' from the Device Settings menu.
5. On the Firmware Update screen, verify that the Charge Case (CC) is listed as a sub-device component.
6. Tap 'Update' or 'Check for Updates' next to the Charge Case (CC) entry.
7. If an update is available, tap 'Start Update' or 'Download & Install' to initiate the DFU process for the CC.
8. Observe the app behavior during the DFU initiation (downloading/installing prompt).
9. Wait 10 seconds and verify the app remains responsive and does not crash.
generated_testcases.expected: Poly Studio Mobile app does not crash, freeze, or become unresponsive when initiating a DFU for the Charge Case sub-device. The app shows a progress indicator or confirmation dialog and remains stable.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CS-001: Verify app does not crash when initiating DFU for Charge Stand (CS) from Device Settings
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android device.
2. Ensure a CS-capable device (e.g., Poly Voyager Focus 2 with Charge Stand) is connected via Bluetooth.
3. Tap the connected [Device Name] to open Device Settings screen.
4. Tap 'Firmware Update' from the Device Settings menu.
5. On the Firmware Update screen, verify that the Charge Stand (CS) is listed as a sub-device component.
6. Tap 'Update' or 'Check for Updates' next to the Charge Stand (CS) entry.
7. If an update is available, tap 'Start Update' or 'Download & Install' to initiate the DFU process for the CS.
8. Observe the app behavior during the DFU initiation.
9. Wait 10 seconds and verify the app remains responsive and does not crash.
generated_testcases.expected: Poly Studio Mobile app does not crash, freeze, or become unresponsive when initiating a DFU for the Charge Stand sub-device. The app shows a progress indicator or confirmation dialog and remains stable.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CC-002: Verify app stability when rapidly navigating to Firmware Update and initiating CC DFU multiple times
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android device.
2. Ensure a CC-capable device (e.g., Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC) is connected via Bluetooth.
3. Tap the connected [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
4. Tap 'Update' next to Charge Case (CC) to initiate DFU.
5. As soon as the DFU initiation dialog appears, tap 'Cancel' or press Back to abort.
6. Immediately tap 'Update' next to Charge Case (CC) again to re-initiate DFU.
7. Repeat steps 5-6 rapidly 5 times in succession.
8. After the 5th attempt, allow the DFU to proceed without cancelling.
9. Observe app behavior throughout the rapid initiation/cancellation cycle and during the final DFU.
generated_testcases.expected: Poly Studio Mobile app does not crash, ANR (Application Not Responding), or freeze during rapid initiation and cancellation of CC DFU. The app remains responsive and the final DFU proceeds normally.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CC-003: Verify app crash recovery when DFU initiation for CC fails mid-process
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android device.
2. Ensure a CC-capable device (e.g., Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC) is connected via Bluetooth.
3. Tap the connected [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
4. Tap 'Update' next to Charge Case (CC) to initiate DFU.
5. During the DFU download/installation phase, force a Bluetooth disconnection by turning off the earbuds or charge case.
6. Wait 15 seconds.
7. Reconnect the device by turning it back on and re-establishing Bluetooth connection.
8. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
9. Observe the app state: check if an error message is displayed, if the DFU status is shown as 'Failed', and if the app is still responsive.
10. Tap 'Retry' or 'Update' next to Charge Case (CC) to attempt DFU again.
generated_testcases.expected: Poly Studio Mobile app does not crash when a CC DFU is interrupted by Bluetooth disconnection. The app gracefully handles the error, displays an appropriate error message (e.g., 'Update failed' or 'Connection lost'), and allows the user to retry the DFU without crashing.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CC-004: Verify app does not crash when initiating CC DFU while audio is streaming
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android device.
2. Ensure a CC-capable device (e.g., Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC) is connected via Bluetooth.
3. Start media playback (e.g., music or podcast) on the connected device.
4. While audio is streaming, open Poly Studio Mobile app.
5. Tap the connected [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
6. Tap 'Update' next to Charge Case (CC) to initiate DFU.
7. Observe app behavior during DFU initiation while audio is actively streaming.
8. Verify that the app does not crash and that audio continues to play or is gracefully paused/handled.
generated_testcases.expected: Poly Studio Mobile app does not crash when initiating a CC DFU while audio is streaming. The app remains stable and responsive. Audio may continue playing or be paused as part of the DFU process, but the app does not force-close or become unresponsive.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CS-002: Verify app does not crash when initiating CS DFU and then immediately backgrounding the app
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android device.
2. Ensure a CS-capable device (e.g., Poly Voyager Focus 2 with Charge Stand) is connected via Bluetooth.
3. Tap the connected [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
4. Tap 'Update' next to Charge Stand (CS) to initiate DFU.
5. Immediately after tapping 'Start Update', press the Home button to background the Poly Studio Mobile app.
6. Wait 10 seconds.
7. Reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app from the app switcher or home screen.
8. Observe the app state: check if the app shows the DFU progress, an error, or if it has crashed.
9. Navigate to [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update to verify the DFU status.
generated_testcases.expected: Poly Studio Mobile app does not crash when backgrounded immediately after initiating a CS DFU. Upon returning to the app, it remains stable and either shows the ongoing DFU progress or an appropriate status message. The app does not force-close or show a blank screen.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CC-005: Verify app stability when initiating CC DFU at low battery on charge case
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android device.
2. Ensure a CC-capable device (e.g., Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC) is connected via Bluetooth.
3. Verify that the Charge Case battery level is low (below 15%) via the app's Device screen.
4. Tap the connected [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
5. Tap 'Update' next to Charge Case (CC) to initiate DFU.
6. Observe if the app displays a low battery warning dialog before proceeding.
7. If a warning is shown, tap 'Continue' or 'Update Anyway' to proceed.
8. Observe app behavior during DFU initiation under low battery condition.
generated_testcases.expected: Poly Studio Mobile app does not crash when initiating a CC DFU with low charge case battery. The app either displays an appropriate low battery warning and allows the user to proceed, or gracefully blocks the update with a clear message. In either case, the app remains stable and responsive.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CC-006: Verify app does not crash when switching between sub-device DFU tabs (CC vs Earbuds) during update
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android device.
2. Ensure a CC-capable device (e.g., Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC) is connected via Bluetooth.
3. Tap the connected [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
4. Tap 'Update' next to Charge Case (CC) to initiate CC DFU.
5. While the CC DFU is in progress (downloading/installing), tap on the Earbuds/Headset tab or entry in the Firmware Update screen.
6. Tap back on the Charge Case (CC) tab/entry.
7. Repeat steps 5-6 rapidly 3 times.
8. Observe app behavior during tab switching while DFU is active.
generated_testcases.expected: Poly Studio Mobile app does not crash, freeze, or ANR when switching between sub-device tabs (CC vs Earbuds) during an active CC DFU. The app remains responsive and the DFU progress is correctly displayed when returning to the CC tab.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CC-007: Verify app stability when initiating DFU for CC immediately after app force-kill and relaunch
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android device.
2. Ensure a CC-capable device (e.g., Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC) is connected via Bluetooth.
3. Tap the connected [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
4. Tap 'Update' next to Charge Case (CC) to initiate DFU.
5. During the DFU initiation, force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app from the Android app settings or recent apps menu.
6. Wait 5 seconds.
7. Relaunch the Poly Studio Mobile app.
8. Ensure the device reconnects automatically or manually reconnect via Bluetooth settings.
9. Navigate to [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
10. Tap 'Update' next to Charge Case (CC) to initiate DFU again.
11. Observe app behavior during the second DFU initiation after force-kill.
generated_testcases.expected: Poly Studio Mobile app does not crash when initiating a CC DFU after a previous force-kill during DFU. The app recovers cleanly, shows the correct DFU state, and allows a fresh DFU initiation without crashing or showing stale state.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CS-003: Verify app does not crash when initiating CS DFU while a call is in progress
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android device.
2. Ensure a CS-capable device (e.g., Poly Voyager Focus 2 with Charge Stand) is connected via Bluetooth.
3. Place or receive an active call on the connected headset.
4. While the call is active, open Poly Studio Mobile app.
5. Tap the connected [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
6. Tap 'Update' next to Charge Stand (CS) to initiate DFU.
7. Observe app behavior during DFU initiation while a call is active.
8. Verify that the app does not crash and that the call is handled gracefully (either continues or is put on hold with user notification).
generated_testcases.expected: Poly Studio Mobile app does not crash when initiating a CS DFU while a call is in progress. The app remains stable. The call may be put on hold or ended as part of the DFU process, but the app does not force-close or become unresponsive.
